problem.title: Maximal Rectangle
problem.description: Given a binary matrix filled with '0's and '1's, find the largest rectangle containing only '1's and return its area.
assumptions: m and n are at least 1.

example.explanation: The largest rectangle of '1's has area 6 (3 columns × 2 rows in the middle region).

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Stack (Histogram per Row)
solutions.description: Build a running height array where heights[j] is the number of consecutive '1's ending at the current row in column j. For each row, apply the classic 'largest rectangle in histogram' algorithm using a monotonic stack to compute the maximum rectangle that can be formed with that row as the bottom boundary. The key insight is that the 2D rectangle problem decomposes row-by-row into 1D histogram problems.

complexity.time: O(mn) — O(n) histogram pass per row, m rows
complexity.space: O(n) for heights array and stack

example_walkthrough.text: After row 2 (index 2), heights = [3,1,3,3,3]. The histogram algorithm finds the rectangle of height 3 spanning columns 2-4 (width=3), giving area 9. But the actual answer from row 1 heights=[2,0,2,2,2] gives the 2×3 rectangle of area 6 at columns 2-4. Scanning all rows, the maximum found is 6.
